faqs.question: Can I use AI to handle creating internal training videos with avatar presenters without losing the core detail?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you choose the tool that matches the input type and then review the output yourself. Synthesia is the best starting point, HeyGen is the strongest second choice, and Canva Magic Studio is the safer fallback when you need a different balance of speed and control.

faqs.question: Which AI tool is better when I need source checking?
faqs.answer: HeyGen is usually the better choice when source checking matters, while Synthesia is stronger when the job starts with a known set of files or notes.

faqs.question: Is Synthesia better than HeyGen for this workflow?
faqs.answer: Synthesia is better when you want the most direct match for the main input; HeyGen is better when the workflow changes enough to justify a different tool.

faqs.question: What should I avoid before I share the result?
faqs.answer: Avoid sharing the first draft until you have checked facts, tone, and any missing context. Use Canva Magic Studio as a helper, not a replacement for review.
